# 54 Patterns for Building Better MCP Tools > Raw source preserved verbatim. Do not edit this file. > Compiled into wiki articles during the ingest stage. --- ## Core Design Philosophy "Your agents are only as good as your tools." Unlike traditional integration patterns where middleware orchestrates workflows, agent-based tools must be self-explanatory because LLMs autonomously decide which tools to call and how to interpret results. ## Classification Framework Tools exist across three dimensions: 1. Maturity: Atomic (single operation) to Orchestrated (coordinated workflows) 2. Integration Type: APIs, databases, file systems, or system operations 3. Access Pattern: Synchronous, asynchronous, streaming, or event-driven ## Four Cross-Cutting Concerns ### Agent Experience Design descriptions, parameter names, and error messages for LLM comprehension, not […]
